implementation_summary.scope_confirmation: No production file was created or modified by this story. Every data-testid named in the story brief (msg-<id>, edited-marker-<id>, msg-edit-<id>, msg-delete-<id>, msg-delete-confirm-row-<id>, msg-delete-confirm-<id>, msg-delete-cancel-<id>, chat-edit-banner, chat-edit-cancel, chat-edit-empty-hint, chat-input, chat-send-btn, image-attachment-button, image-preview-thumbnail, image-thumbnail) already existed in app/src/components/chat/ChatBox.tsx from S6 — confirmed by direct read before delegating implementation, and independently confirmed again in review. The only production-adjacent finding is that ChatBox.tsx does NOT emit a literal msg-deleted-<id> testid: a tombstoned row is filtered out of visibleMessages entirely (AC-DEL-3: silent removal, no placeholder), so both spec files use the disappearance of msg-<id> (expect.poll to count 0) as the delete-readiness signal instead of adding a new testid for a marker that would contradict the 'no placeholder' requirement. See VQ-S7-009.
implementation_summary.dm_coverage: app/tests/e2e/dm-message-edit-delete.spec.ts (9 tests, test.describe.serial with a single Alice/Bob walled-garden DM setup in beforeAll via createGroupAndInvite, mirroring dm-self-heal.spec.ts / groups-dm-reactions.spec.ts). Covers AC-DEL-1 (text + image) with AC-DEL-3's cross-peer disappearance; AC-EDIT-1/AC-EDIT-2/AC-EDIT-3 (in-place update + edited marker, both sides); AC-AUTH-1 (action buttons not-attached on a peer's message, attached-after-hover on own); AC-DEL-6 (single click arms, does not delete; second/confirm click does); AC-EDIT-5 (empty edit blocked + hint + disabled Save, cancel restores original untouched); AC-IMG-2 (image message: delete attached, edit not attached); an S3-owned confirmatory test (reactions survive an edit, vanish on delete, via the pre-existing __fewDmReactions bridge for the reaction half only); and an S3-owned AC-ORDER-1/AC-ORDER-3 e2e proxy (documented in-code as a best-effort proxy — Playwright cannot dictate relay wire-delivery order; the test instead controls Bob's local ingest timing by moving him off the DM route before Alice sends+deletes, so both signals are already resolvable on his FIRST fetch of the thread, then sustains a poll asserting the message never renders).
implementation_summary.group_coverage: app/tests/e2e/groups-message-edit-delete.spec.ts (7 tests, test.describe.serial with a single group-create/invite/accept setup in beforeAll, mirroring groups-reactions.spec.ts's story-06 describe block). Covers the same AC-DEL-1 (text + image) / AC-EDIT-1/2/3 / AC-AUTH-1 / AC-DEL-6 / AC-EDIT-5 / AC-IMG-2 set, adapted to the group MLS flow. Per the brief's allowance ('you do not need to duplicate the ordering/reactions-survive confirmatory tests in BOTH files if time is tight'), the ordering and reactions-survive/vanish confirmatory tests were placed only in the DM file (judged more reliable to set up for that specific pair of assertions); AC-DEL-1 and AC-EDIT-1 themselves are covered in BOTH transports without exception, which is this story's actual owned scope.
implementation_summary.ui_vs_bridge_choice: All 16 new tests drive the real UI for the delete/edit action itself (hover -> msg-edit-<id>/msg-delete-<id> click -> confirm/save), not the window.__fewDmMessageEdits/__fewGroupMessageEdits dev bridge — this closes S6's own flagged gap (VQ-S6-023: JSX wiring for the action menu was previously unverifiable by any automated test, given this project's no-jsdom convention). The pre-existing __fewDmReactions/__fewReactions bridges are used only for the reaction HALF of the reactions-survive-edit confirmatory test, where the reaction picker UI itself is out of this story's scope.
implementation_summary.run_command_correction: The story brief assumed DM specs run via the non-relay make test-e2e-fast bucket. app/playwright.config.ts's actual testMatch/testIgnore split routes ANY dm-*.spec.ts or groups-*.spec.ts filename into the relay-required ('groups', E2E_GROUPS=1) mode — DM message edit/delete needs the same walled-garden MLS group prerequisite as the pre-existing dm-*/groups-dm-* specs, so it is not eligible for the fast bucket regardless of filename. Both new files are named accordingly and were run exclusively via `E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs ...` against the Docker-based strfry+blossom-mock harness (make e2e-up / make e2e-down). See VQ-S7-013.

coverage: AC-DEL-1 (text + image), DM and group: authored message deleted via real UI, disappears for author AND peer (msg-<id> not attached)
AC-DEL-3: cross-peer silent removal — peer's bubble is confirmed visible before delete, then confirmed gone after

AC-EDIT-1/AC-EDIT-2/AC-EDIT-3, DM and group: authored text message edited via real UI, updates in place (same slot testid, old content gone, new content present) with edited-marker-<id> visible on both sides
AC-AUTH-1 (confirmatory), DM and group: action buttons not-attached on a peer's message, attached (post-hover) on the author's own
AC-DEL-6 (confirmatory), DM and group: single click arms the confirm row without deleting; only the confirm click deletes
AC-EDIT-5 (confirmatory), DM and group: whitespace-only edit content shows chat-edit-empty-hint and disables chat-send-btn; cancel restores the untouched original
AC-IMG-2 (confirmatory), DM and group: image message offers msg-delete-<id> but not msg-edit-<id>
S3-owned confirmatory (DM only): reactions survive a real-UI edit and vanish on a real-UI delete
S3-owned confirmatory (DM only): AC-ORDER-1/AC-ORDER-3 outcome proxy — a message deleted before the peer's first-ever thread open never renders for that peer, sustained poll over 8s

gates.unit_test_status: PASS (129 files, 2399 tests, unchanged from baseline — this story touches no unit-tested code path)
gates.lint_status: NO_LINT (unchanged from baseline — no eslint config present in this project)

e2e_new_specs.command: E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs tests/e2e/dm-message-edit-delete.spec.ts tests/e2e/groups-message-edit-delete.spec.ts (after make e2e-up)
e2e_new_specs.status: PASS — 16/16 (1.1m). Run twice: once by the implementing dev agent, once independently re-run by the architect with a fresh docker compose harness (both runs green, no flakes observed).
e2e_new_specs.note: Both files require the Docker-based strfry relay + blossom mock (E2E_GROUPS=1 mode) despite one being named 'dm-*' — see run_command_correction above and VQ-S7-013.

e2e_regression_spot_check.status: PASS — 14/14 (56.9s). Run by the architect after the new specs landed, to confirm the two new files and the S1-S6 testid/dev-bridge additions did not disturb the pre-existing reaction specs that share the same ChatBox testids and dev-bridge pattern.
e2e_regression_spot_check.note: This is a targeted spot check, NOT the full 48-test e2e gate. make test-e2e-all (48 tests: 10 non-relay + 38 relay) remains the lead's responsibility per this story's scope.excludes and the project's E2E gate convention (CLAUDE.md).

e2e_full_suite.status: NOT RUN by this story — deliberately out of scope. Both required buckets (the new specs' bucket and the regression spot-check) were run and are green; the full 48-test make test-e2e-all gate is the lead's responsibility after this story lands, per the story brief ('the lead runs the full make test-e2e-all gate afterward') and CLAUDE.md's E2E gate section.

verification_json.note: Pre-impl questions VQ-S7-001..008 left untouched per the immutability rule. Appended VQ-S7-009 (msg-deleted-<id> substitution via msg-<id> disappearance, given AC-DEL-3's no-placeholder requirement means no positive marker can legitimately exist), VQ-S7-010 (honesty of the AC-ORDER-1/AC-ORDER-3 e2e proxy given Playwright cannot control wire delivery order), VQ-S7-011 (toBeAttached vs toBeVisible for AC-AUTH-1's positive/negative cases given ChatBox's showActions-gates-the-whole-block vs _groupHover-opacity-toggle distinction), VQ-S7-012 (does this story's real-UI-driven testing close S6's own flagged VQ-S6-023 JSX-wiring gap), VQ-S7-013 (correcting the story brief's fast-bucket assumption against playwright.config.ts's actual dm-*/groups-* relay-mode testMatch, with independent re-run evidence).

gate_remediation.trigger: Two independent reviews confirmed S7's OWNED ACs (AC-DEL-1, AC-EDIT-1) solidly covered; findings were on the confirmatory ordering-proxy test and test-helper robustness. Test-code-only hardening, no production changes.
findings_addressed: sev4 ordering-proxy vacuous-pass window: added a second NOT-deleted control message sent in the same window; after Bob's fresh mount, first assert the control message renders (positive proof this mount's ingest actually works), then run the deleted-id absence check — so a broken fresh-mount ingest or a send that never reached the relay now fails the test instead of passing vacuously.

sev3 sampled-absence guarantee: replaced the 500ms-interval sampling loop with a MutationObserver installed via page.addInitScript on Bob's next navigation, latching true the moment [data-testid="msg-<id>"] for the deleted message ever attaches (childList + attribute observation on document.documentElement). expectMessageGone(pageB, id) now serves as the sustained-poll window and the latch is asserted false afterward — a gap-free never-attached guarantee instead of point-in-time sampling.

sev3 fixed 3s settle wait: kept (no clean pre-open relay-resolvability readiness marker exists in this codebase — confirmed by search), but rewrote the in-code comment to document the residual explicitly: scopes the e2e's claim to 'fresh-mount batch ingest converges to deleted', notes AC-ORDER-1/AC-ORDER-3 are owned and deterministically tested by S3, and ties the wait to the AC-60 relay-propagation-allowance precedent so a future flake is diagnosed against that precedent rather than re-litigated.

sev2 image helper before-set guard: sendDmImageAndGetMessageId (dm) and sendGroupImageAndGetId (group) now snapshot existing msg-* testids before send and assert the resolved id is NOT in that before-set, mirroring the text helpers (sendDmAndGetMessageId / sendGroupMessageAndGetId).

sev2 edit tests missing pre-edit assertions: both files' AC-EDIT-1/2/3 test now asserts, before editMessageViaUi runs, that both Alice's and Bob's bubbles contain the original content and that edited-marker-<id> is not attached on either side — completing the 'old content there, then replaced, marker appears' narrative within the test.
sev1 unused import: removed the unused dismissErrorOverlay import from dm-message-edit-delete.spec.ts (groups-message-edit-delete.spec.ts already uses it and was left unchanged).
gate_remediation.left_as_is: The two waitForTimeout(5_000) calls in groups-message-edit-delete.spec.ts's beforeAll (KeyPackage/invite propagation) — inherited verbatim from groups-reactions.spec.ts's template; changing them would diverge from the sibling spec's setup pattern rather than fix a test-quality gap.

verification_run.command: make e2e-up && E2E_GROUPS=1 node scripts/run-e2e.mjs tests/e2e/dm-message-edit-delete.spec.ts tests/e2e/groups-message-edit-delete.spec.ts && make e2e-down
verification_run.status: PASS — 16/16 (1.0m), Docker available, run after all six findings were addressed.
verification_run.typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (from app/) — clean, no new errors.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The story brief and stories.json assumed the DM e2e specs would run in the non-relay make test-e2e-fast bucket, but app/playwright.config.ts routes any dm-*/groups-* filename to the Docker/relay-required bucket — DM edit/delete also needs a walled-garden MLS group. Caught before wasted effort (both specs correctly need Docker), but the brief's fast-bucket assumption cost a diagnostic detour. Future e2e briefs should state that dm-*/groups-* both require the relay bucket.
retrospective.surprised_by: The story brief (and stories.json's own text) assumed DM specs are eligible for the non-relay make test-e2e-fast bucket. app/playwright.config.ts's testMatch/testIgnore split is by filename prefix only (dm-*.spec.ts / groups-*.spec.ts -> relay mode, everything else -> fast mode) and DM delete/edit needs the same walled-garden MLS group prerequisite the pre-existing dm-*/groups-dm-* specs already need, so no DM spec touching this feature can ever run in the fast bucket. This was caught before it caused a wasted run (both new files were correctly named and run via E2E_GROUPS=1 from the start), but is worth correcting in the epic's own docs for future reference — see VQ-S7-013.
